2606.21820.title: Generating Public Health Responses using Survey-Augmented Large Language Models
2606.21820.paper_summary: Epidemiological models often rely on survey data to represent how individuals make health-related decisions, such as whether to vaccinate or adopt protective behaviors. However, repeated large-scale surveys are costly, time-consuming, and limited in the range of scenarios they can capture. In this work, we investigate whether large language models (LLMs) can generate synthetic survey responses that reproduce patterns observed in real populations. Using longitudinal data from the FluPaths surveys, we first identify groups associated with broadly positive or negative attitudes toward vaccination through clustering analysis. We then evaluate several LLMs using a cluster-informed prompting approach to generate synthetic survey responses across multiple epidemic waves. Across models, the synthetic data generally reproduce the distributions of demographic characteristics, vaccination-related beliefs, risk perceptions, and health behaviors observed in the survey data. However, they are less successful at capturing how these factors vary together within respondents. Some models reproduce group-level vaccination trends more reliably than others, although performance varies across waves. We also trained a classifier to distinguish real from synthetic records and found that the generated responses remained identifiable as synthetic. Overall, our findings suggest that LLM-generated survey data may provide a useful tool for exploratory data augmentation and we hope that it could support agent-based epidemic modeling approaches. However, the generated data should not be treated as a substitute for human survey data without further methodological improvements and validation.
2606.21820.paper_summary_zh: 流行病學模型通常依賴調查數據來表示個體如何做出與健康相關的決策，例如是否接種疫苗或採取保護行為。<lb/>然而，重複的大規模調查成本高昂、耗時且在能捕捉的場景範圍上有限。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們研究大型語言模型（LLMs）是否能生成合成調查回應，以重現在真實人群中觀察到的模式。<lb/>利用來自 FluPaths 調查的縱向數據，我們首先通過聚類分析識別與對疫苗接種持有廣泛正面或負面態度相關的群體。<lb/>然後，我們使用基於聚類的提示方法評估幾個 LLM，以在多個流行病浪潮中生成合成調查回應。<lb/>在各模型中，合成數據通常重現了調查數據中觀察到的人口特徵、與疫苗接種相關的信念、風險感知和健康行為的分佈。<lb/>然而，它們在捕捉這些因素如何在受訪者中共同變化方面的成功率較低。<lb/>某些模型比其他模型更可靠地重現群體層級的疫苗接種趨勢，儘管性能在不同浪潮中有所變化。<lb/>我們還訓練了一個分類器來區分真實記錄和合成記錄，發現生成的回應仍然可以識別為合成的。<lb/>總體而言，我們的發現表明，LLM生成的調查數據可能為探索性數據增強提供有用的工具，我們希望它能支持基於代理的流行病建模方法。<lb/>然而，生成的數據在進一步的方法改進和驗證之前，不應被視為人類調查數據的替代品。
2606.21820.author: Leonardo Marciaga et.al.
2606.21820.authors: Leonardo Marciaga, Thuyen Pham, Julia Rezvani, Alina Hyk, Chunyang Liao, Konstantinos Mitsopoulos, Raffaele Vardavas
